name: away_natvent_activates_free_cooling
description: Away mode, warm day: a window opens while away with outdoor (68F) cooler than indoor (74F). Natural ventilation activates — free cooling is free cooling, home or away. The user opts into fan-assisted ventilation by configuring a fan; occupancy does not gate it. #231 ensures nat-vent exits at the comfort ceiling so an empty home is not over-cooled below comfort.
notes: Occupant outcome: the home pre-cools for free off the open window + configured fan while away, cutting the AC load on return. The user chose this by configuring a fan; not configuring a fan is how a user opts out of nat-vent.
Design note: nat-vent activation is gated on fan configuration + temperature, NOT occupancy. (An earlier change, #254, wrongly added an occupancy block; reverted — fan config is the opt-in.)
Regression proof: if an occupancy guard is (re-)added to nat-vent activation, the 14:00 assertion flips from 'natural_ventilation' to 'paused' and this scenario fails.

events.note: Afternoon: indoor 74F, outdoor 68F — nat-vent eligible by temperature (outdoor < indoor, indoor > comfort_heat)
Window opens while away with cool outdoor air — nat-vent activates (free cooling); occupancy does not block it

assertions.reason: sensor_open while away with outdoor (68F) < indoor (74F) and a fan configured — natural ventilation activates for free cooling. Occupancy does not gate nat-vent; the user opts in/out via fan configuration. #231 handles the comfort-ceiling exit so the empty home is not over-cooled.

verdict.summary: Nat-vent activates while away for free cooling — fan configuration is the opt-in, not occupancy; #231 exits at the comfort ceiling
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied -> away setback (79F) -> natural_ventilation on sensor_open at 14:00 (free cooling)
verdict.expected_behavior: An open window with cool outdoor air activates fan-assisted ventilation regardless of occupancy; users opt out by not configuring a fan
